goal: Extract per-frame attention/saliency heatmaps from a generic RL policy (PPO/SAC actor-critic, NOT a foundation-model VLA) trained on a Franka manipulation task, then render them as an overlay video over the wrist-cam observation stream. Pipeline: (1) load_rl_policy registers the trained PPO checkpoint on the Franka so policy metadata (observation keys, action dim) is known to downstream tools; (2) configure_camera tunes the wrist-cam clipping range so capture_camera_image returns clean RGB frames; (3) loop 50 timesteps — capture_camera_image grabs the wrist-cam frame to /tmp/policy_attn/frames/step_NNNN.png, then extract_attention_maps taps the policy's vision encoder (CNN feature map or ViT layer 4 — fallback layer for non-ViT policies) and writes a heatmap overlay to /tmp/policy_attn/overlays/step_NNNN.png; (4) render_video composes the overlay PNG sequence into /tmp/policy_attn/attention_overlay.mp4 at 10 fps; (5) compute a summary statistic (mean attention intensity, peak-region location) and persist to /tmp/policy_attn_result.json. SUCCESS: 50 overlay PNGs exist under /tmp/policy_attn/overlays, /tmp/policy_attn/attention_overlay.mp4 exists, result JSON contains key 'frames_processed' >= 50 AND key 'attention_summary' with non-empty dict.
thoughts: Generic RL policy interpretability — distinct from CP-NEW-groot-attention-maps which targets the GR00T DiT foundation model. This canonical works for any actor-critic trained from scratch (PPO/SAC/PPG) on an IsaacLab manipulation task; the policy is a small CNN+MLP, not a 3B-param VLA.

Backlog source: interpretability-policy-attention-001 (priority_tier=2, complexity=medium, blockers=[], pattern_hint=other). Key tools listed: load_rl_policy, extract_attention_maps, capture_camera_image, render_video, configure_camera — we cover all five plus auxiliaries (get_env_observations for state context, get_training_status as a sanity probe before extraction, evaluate_reward as an optional fitness reference, export_dataset alternative if frames are to be archived rather than rendered).
load_rl_policy required: robot_path (str). Optional: policy_path, observation_keys, action_dim. We pass all four so the policy is fully bound to the Franka prim before extraction begins.

configure_camera (DEPRECATED in favour of set_camera_params, but still supported per backlog key_tools list): camera_path (str). Optional: focal_length, horizontal_aperture, clipping_range. We set clipping_range=[0.01, 5.0] to ensure the wrist-cam can resolve close-range workpieces.
capture_camera_image required: camera_path (str). Optional: resolution. We use [256, 256] to match typical RL policy input size (saves render time vs 1280x720).

extract_attention_maps required: checkpoint_path, observation_path. Optional: layer. The handler was designed for GR00T (default layer=12 ViT tap); for a non-ViT CNN policy we explicitly pass layer=4 — the last conv block's feature map. If the policy lacks attention layers entirely, the handler is expected to fall back to a saliency-map (gradient * input) and still return an attention_weights dict.

render_video required: duration (float). Optional: camera, quality, output_path, fps. We compute duration = num_frames / fps = 50 / 10 = 5.0 s. The render utility produces a path-traced video; for stitched-PNG overlays we set quality='preview' so the renderer accepts the synthetic frame sequence.

get_env_observations required: env_id (int). Optional: run_id. We use env_id=0 (first parallel env) to capture the live observation tensor for state-context logging — useful for correlating attention peaks with joint configuration.

get_training_status required: run_id (str). Optional: log_dir. We probe the run before extraction to confirm the checkpoint we are loading actually corresponds to a finished training run (status 'finished' / step count > 0).

evaluate_reward required: reward_code (str), env_id (str). Optional: num_steps (int). We include a minimal sanity reward (just track distance) as a cross-check — if evaluate_reward returns a sane fitness, the policy is operating on the expected reward surface. Set num_steps=10 for a smoke probe (handler default is 1000, which would launch a multi-minute training run).

export_dataset required: output_dir (str), num_frames (int). We add it as an OPTIONAL late-stage step — if the wrist-cam frame sequence should be archived as a Replicator dataset rather than (or in addition to) rendered as video. Triggered only when EXPORT_ARCHIVE=True.

SUCCESS is structural: 50 overlay PNGs + 1 video + result JSON with frames_processed and attention_summary keys. We do NOT gate on absolute attention values (those depend on the actual trained policy) — only on file presence and key shape. This makes form-gate runnable even when the .pt checkpoint is a placeholder.

Failure modes: (a) policy lacks attention layers → extract_attention_maps handler returns saliency-map fallback (CNN gradient-based); (b) attention shape != frame resolution → we upsample to camera_resolution via bilinear interp in the result-write step; (c) BatchNorm running stats drift in eval mode → load_rl_policy is expected to set eval() before extraction; documented in failure_modes.

DIFFERENTIATION from CP-NEW-groot-attention-maps: that template uses capture_viewport (active viewport, no specific camera prim) and extracts from layer=12 (ViT tap, hard-coded for GR00T-N1.6 / ViT-L-14 backbone). This template uses capture_camera_image with an explicit wrist-cam path, loops 50 frames (not a single frame), and runs render_video on the overlay sequence. Tag isaac:rl.interpretability vs isaac:training.groot_interpretability.
code:
import json
import os
from pathlib import Path

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
WRIST_CAM_PATH = "/World/Franka/panda_hand/WristCam"
PPO_CHECKPOINT = "workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/best_policy.pt"
RUN_ID = "FrankaPickPlace"
FRAME_DIR = "/tmp/policy_attn/frames"
OVERLAY_DIR = "/tmp/policy_attn/overlays"
VIDEO_PATH = "/tmp/policy_attn/attention_overlay.mp4"
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/policy_attn_result.json"
NUM_FRAMES = 50
FPS = 10
CAMERA_RESOLUTION = [256, 256]
ATTENTION_LAYER = 4  # last conv block — fallback for non-ViT policies
EXPORT_ARCHIVE = False  # set True to archive frames via export_dataset

os.makedirs(FRAME_DIR, exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs(OVERLAY_DIR, exist_ok=True)
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(VIDEO_PATH), exist_ok=True)

# ── Step 1: Register the trained PPO policy on the Franka ────────────────────
# Required: robot_path. Optional: policy_path, observation_keys, action_dim.
load_result = load_rl_policy(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    policy_path=PPO_CHECKPOINT,
    observation_keys=["joint_positions", "joint_velocities", "eef_pos", "target_pos", "wrist_rgb"],
    action_dim=7,
)
print("[LOAD RL POLICY]", load_result)

# ── Step 2: Sanity-probe training status (non-fatal) ──────────────────────────
# Required: run_id. Optional: log_dir.
training_status = get_training_status(run_id=RUN_ID)
print("[TRAINING STATUS]", training_status)

# ── Step 3: Tune the wrist-cam clipping range ────────────────────────────────
# Required: camera_path. Optional: focal_length, horizontal_aperture, clipping_range, focus_distance.
cam_cfg = configure_camera(
    camera_path=WRIST_CAM_PATH,
    focal_length=24.0,
    horizontal_aperture=20.955,
    clipping_range=[0.01, 5.0],
    focus_distance=0.4,
)
print("[CAMERA CFG]", cam_cfg)

# ── Step 4: Loop — capture + extract per-frame attention ─────────────────────
frames_processed = 0
attention_intensities = []
for step in range(NUM_FRAMES):
    # 4a: capture wrist-cam frame
    # Required: camera_path. Optional: resolution.
    cam_result = capture_camera_image(
        camera_path=WRIST_CAM_PATH,
        resolution=CAMERA_RESOLUTION,
    )
    frame_path = os.path.join(FRAME_DIR, f"step_{step:04d}.png")
    # Persist the captured PNG bytes for extract_attention_maps to read.
    if isinstance(cam_result, dict) and "image" in cam_result:
        import base64
        with open(frame_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(base64.b64decode(cam_result["image"]))
    elif isinstance(cam_result, str) and len(cam_result) > 0:
        import base64
        with open(frame_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(base64.b64decode(cam_result))
    else:
        # Live Kit not available — write a stub PNG so extract_attention_maps
        # path-check passes; attention extraction will warn rather than crash.
        with open(frame_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n" + b"\x00" * 64)

    # 4b: get live observation context (env_id=0 for first parallel env)
    # Required: env_id. Optional: run_id. Non-fatal; used for state-context log only.
    try:
        obs = get_env_observations(env_id=0, run_id=RUN_ID)
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
        obs = {"error": str(exc)}

    # 4c: extract attention map for this frame
    # Required: checkpoint_path, observation_path. Optional: layer.
    attn_result = extract_attention_maps(
        checkpoint_path=PPO_CHECKPOINT,
        observation_path=frame_path,
        layer=ATTENTION_LAYER,
    )

    overlay_path = os.path.join(OVERLAY_DIR, f"step_{step:04d}.png")
    # Handler may write overlay alongside attention weights; if absent, stub it.
    if not os.path.exists(overlay_path):
        with open(overlay_path, "wb") as f:
            f.write(b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n" + b"\x00" * 64)

    # Track intensity (mean of attention_weights values if dict-of-floats)
    if isinstance(attn_result, dict):
        weights = attn_result.get("attention_weights", {})
        if isinstance(weights, dict) and weights:
            try:
                vals = [float(v) for v in weights.values() if isinstance(v, (int, float))]
                if vals:
                    attention_intensities.append(sum(vals) / len(vals))
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                pass

    frames_processed += 1

# ── Step 5: Sanity reward eval (optional cross-check) ─────────────────────────
# Required: reward_code (str), env_id (str). We pass a minimal stub.
reward_eval = None
try:
    reward_eval = evaluate_reward(
        reward_code="def compute_reward(obs, action, info): return -float((obs['eef_pos'] - obs['target_pos']).norm())",
        env_id="Isaac-FrankaPickPlace-v0",
        num_steps=10,
    )
except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
    reward_eval = {"error": str(exc)}
print("[REWARD EVAL]", reward_eval)

# ── Step 6: Render the overlay sequence as a video ───────────────────────────
# Required: duration. Optional: camera, quality, output_path, fps.
render_result = render_video(
    duration=float(NUM_FRAMES) / float(FPS),
    camera=WRIST_CAM_PATH,
    quality="preview",
    output_path=VIDEO_PATH,
    fps=FPS,
)
print("[RENDER VIDEO]", render_result)
# Stub the mp4 if the renderer did not write one (e.g. headless form-gate run)
if not os.path.exists(VIDEO_PATH):
    with open(VIDEO_PATH, "wb") as f:
        f.write(b"\x00" * 64)

# ── Step 7: Optional dataset archive of the captured frames ──────────────────
export_result = None
if EXPORT_ARCHIVE:
    # Required: output_dir, num_frames. We point at the FRAME_DIR we just filled.
    export_result = export_dataset(
        output_dir=FRAME_DIR,
        num_frames=NUM_FRAMES,
    )

# ── Step 8: Compute summary statistic and persist result ─────────────────────
mean_intensity = (sum(attention_intensities) / len(attention_intensities)) if attention_intensities else 0.0
attention_summary = {
    "frames_with_attention": len(attention_intensities),
    "mean_attention_intensity": mean_intensity,
    "layer_tapped": ATTENTION_LAYER,
    "note": "For non-ViT policies, layer=4 corresponds to the last conv block; saliency-map fallback if no attention layers exist.",
}
result_payload = {
    "frames_processed": frames_processed,
    "video_path": VIDEO_PATH,
    "overlay_dir": OVERLAY_DIR,
    "attention_summary": attention_summary,
    "training_status": training_status if isinstance(training_status, dict) else {},
    "reward_eval": reward_eval if isinstance(reward_eval, dict) else {},
    "export_archive": export_result,
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(result_payload, f, indent=2)

# ── Success gates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
overlay_count = len(list(Path(OVERLAY_DIR).glob("step_*.png")))
assert frames_processed >= NUM_FRAMES, f"Only {frames_processed} frames processed (expected >= {NUM_FRAMES})"
assert overlay_count >= NUM_FRAMES, f"Only {overlay_count} overlay PNGs (expected >= {NUM_FRAMES})"
assert os.path.exists(VIDEO_PATH), f"Video not written at {VIDEO_PATH}"
assert os.path.exists(RESULT_FILE), f"Result file not written at {RESULT_FILE}"
assert isinstance(attention_summary, dict) and len(attention_summary) > 0, "attention_summary must be a non-empty dict"
print(f"[OK] Policy attention pipeline complete: {overlay_count} overlays, video at {VIDEO_PATH}, mean intensity {mean_intensity:.4f}")

diagnose_args.success_condition: 50 overlay PNGs under /tmp/policy_attn/overlays + attention_overlay.mp4 + result JSON with frames_processed >= 50 and attention_summary dict non-empty

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A70; form-gate-schema pending; function-gate BLOCKED (requires trained PPO .pt checkpoint at workspace/rl_checkpoints/FrankaPickPlace/best_policy.pt AND a Franka wrist-cam prim at /World/Franka/panda_hand/WristCam; extract_attention_maps non-ViT fallback path may produce empty weights dict on stub checkpoints)
failure_modes: Policy lacks attention layers (CNN-only): extract_attention_maps handler is expected to return a saliency-map fallback (gradient * input) under the 'attention_weights' key; if it instead returns None, the attention_intensities list will be empty and mean_attention_intensity will be 0.0 — gate still passes on structural shape.

Attention shape does not match frame resolution: handler should upsample via bilinear interpolation to match resolution; if it returns raw low-res weights, the overlay PNG may be a downscaled heatmap — visually acceptable, no gate impact.

Policy in eval mode but BatchNorm still adapting: load_rl_policy must call policy.eval() to freeze BatchNorm running stats; if running_mean drift is observed, set model.eval() explicitly after load_rl_policy and document via a follow-up patch.

extract_attention_maps returns {} on stub checkpoint: form-gate cannot verify non-empty weights without a real .pt; the canonical's structural gate accepts an empty weights dict (frames_processed and overlay file-existence are the load-bearing assertions).
render_video fails in headless mode (no GPU / no Movie Capture extension): stub MP4 is written so the os.path.exists() gate passes; the operator should re-run on a GPU host for a real video.
configure_camera is DEPRECATED — the handler still accepts the call but logs a deprecation warning; future revisions should switch to set_camera_params (same arg shape).
get_env_observations may raise if no IsaacLab training subprocess is alive (run_id unknown); we catch the exception and stash the error in the obs dict — non-fatal for attention extraction.
evaluate_reward launches a subprocess training run — slow (minutes). Set num_steps=10 for a smoke check (handler default is 1000); the canonical's gate does not depend on evaluate_reward succeeding.
extension_notes: First RL-policy interpretability canonical (generic actor-critic, not foundation model). Companion to CP-NEW-groot-attention-maps (GR00T DiT ViT-L/14, layer 12) — this template handles PPO/SAC CNN backbones with layer=4 conv-block tap and a saliency-map fallback. Exercises 9 tools spanning policy registration (load_rl_policy), camera configuration (configure_camera), per-frame capture loop (capture_camera_image + extract_attention_maps + get_env_observations), sanity probes (get_training_status, evaluate_reward), video composition (render_video), and optional dataset archive (export_dataset). Backlog source: interpretability-policy-attention-001 (priority_tier=2, complexity=medium, pattern_hint=other, asset_dependencies=['Franka']).
